texto: STARS AND DANDELIONSDeep in the blue sky,like pebbles at the bottom of the sea,lie the stars unseen in daylightuntil night comes. You can't see them, but they are there. Unseen things are still there.The withered, seedless dandelionshidden in the cracks of the roof tilewait silently for spring,their strong roots unseen. You can't see them, but they are there. Unseen things are still there.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Misuzu Kaneko: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “STARS AND DANDELIONSDeep in the blue sky,like pebbles at the bottom of the sea,lie the stars unse…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, esperanca, invisibility pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de boa-noite e reflexao-noturna.
